sessions.summary: Initialized project (story-level depth, express RFP tier). Ingested 5 discovery files, reframed as Salesforce opportunity, produced project summary.

rejected_alternatives: Proceed as general scoping
Stop and clarify project fit

sessions.summary: Defined 11 epics (E01-E11) covering public portal, agency portal, contact center, reservation engine, payments, RIDB, security/ATO, data migration, analytics, marketing (optional), training. Installed markitdown[pdf], converted 4 large PDFs (Experience Cloud, Service Cloud, Data Cloud, Agentforce), indexed 2,061 KB sections. Validated epics against KB.

decisions.rationale: Covers all SOO Section 6 performance objectives; clean boundaries; grounded in KB + SOO requirements.
rejected_alternatives: Consolidate Security+DevOps
Split Reservation Engine by inventory type

decisions.decision: Wait for full KB indexing before approving epics
decisions.rationale: User preferred KB-grounded epics over assumptions-based epics.
rejected_alternatives: Approve with partial KB
Skip KB validation

open_threads: Story-level analysis in progress
Marketing Cloud scope TBD (likely optional)
Payment integration pattern needs FSC validation
sessions.next_steps: Spawn epic-analyst agents for story-level deepening (E01-E11)

sessions.summary: Pivoted to design skill. Developed comprehensive Salesforce architecture (single Government Cloud org, Experience Cloud dual community strategy, Service Cloud + Voice, Data Cloud for RIDB, Platform for reservation engine, Shield for PII). Created solution brief (client-facing, epic-by-epic ordered by business process) and architecture reference (internal technical documentation). T-shirt sized all 11 epics: 3 XL (E01 Public Portal, E04 Reservation Engine, E08 Data Migration), 3 L (E02 Agency Portal, E05 Payments, E07 Security/ATO), 4 M (E03 Contact Center, E06 RIDB, E09 Analytics, E11 Training), 1 S (E10 Marketing - likely post-MVP).

decisions.decision: Single org strategy for 8 federal agencies
decisions.rationale: Recreation.gov is unified interagency program with shared reservation inventory; role-based access provides sufficient isolation without multi-org complexity.
rejected_alternatives: Multi-org (1 per agency) - would fragment inventory and complicate reporting
Hybrid (prod single, non-prod multi) - unnecessary for sandbox strategy

decisions.rationale: Point-to-point integrations (Worldpay, Login.gov, US Bank, EMV terminals) are simple enough for direct Apex API calls; Data Cloud handles RIDB aggregation. 3-5 integrations do not justify middleware overhead.
rejected_alternatives: MuleSoft Anypoint - too expensive and complex for MVP scale; re-evaluate at 5+ integrations

decisions.decision: Salesforce DevOps Center for deployment pipeline
decisions.rationale: Bridges gap between Change Sets (no version control) and full CI/CD (complex toolchain). Government ownership requirement (SOO Section 6.1) favors Git; DevOps Center provides this with point-and-click UI.
rejected_alternatives: Change Sets - rejected due to no version control
Copado - too expensive for MVP

decisions.decision: Custom Apex development for lottery system
decisions.rationale: No AppExchange app identified for federal lottery requirements (fairness, audit trails, SOO constraints). Build custom with cryptographically secure random number generation and Platform Events audit trail.
rejected_alternatives: AppExchange raffle apps - evaluated but none meet federal compliance requirements

decisions.decision: Mapbox or ArcGIS custom LWC for 1m GSD satellite imagery
decisions.rationale: Salesforce native maps insufficient for 1-meter Ground Sampling Distance requirement (SOO Section 6.6.5). Custom LWC embeds map tiles; usage-based billing.
rejected_alternatives: Salesforce native maps - rejected due to insufficient imagery resolution
Google Maps - evaluated but Mapbox/ArcGIS preferred for federal use cases

Generated Excel workbook (scoping-deliverables.xlsx) with 2 sheets: Epics and Estimates. No user stories, gaps, roadmap, or efficiency data yet (deferred).

decisions.rationale: Dollar amounts ($100K MuleSoft, $50K Copado) are vendor product cost comparisons in the architecture reference document, which is explicitly marked 'Internal Use Only - Not for Client Distribution'. These are appropriate for internal technical decision-making context.
rejected_alternatives: Sanitize all dollar amounts before export

sessions.summary: Completed story-level requirements analysis. Spawned 11 epic-analyst agents (batched in groups of 6 per user constraint) to generate INVEST user stories + gap analysis. Produced 257 user stories and 103 gaps across all epics.

decisions.rationale: User requested limiting to 6 consecutive agents to avoid token usage. Launched in two batches: first 7 agents, then final 4 agents.
rejected_alternatives: Launch all 11 agents simultaneously (would exceed user's token budget preference)

open_threads: Full JSON consolidation into user-stories.json and gaps.json pending (257 stories, 103 gaps)

EMV terminal vendor selection and API specs (G0503)
Marketing Cloud licensing decision (E10 - likely post-MVP)
Experience Cloud offline capability gap — requires Mobile SDK custom development (G0207 critical)
sessions.next_steps: Consolidate full JSON from task notifications into user-stories.json (257 stories) and gaps.json (103 gaps), then run json-to-csv.py. Present gap summary organized by category.

sessions.summary: Created 4-phase implementation roadmap with sequence-only (no duration commitment). Phase 1: Foundation & Security (E07, E04). Phase 2: Core Reservation & Public Portal (E01, E05, E03). Phase 3: Agency Tools & Data Integration (E02, E06, E08). Phase 4: Analytics, Training & Enhancements (E09, E11, E10 optional). Identified 18 disciplines across architecture, development, security, data, integration, training, and program management.

decisions.rationale: Security-first (Phase 1 ATO + reservation engine foundation), value-early (Phase 2 public MVP), back-office after public-facing validated (Phase 3), analytics after operational data (Phase 4). Balanced complexity: Phase 1 = 1 XL + 1 L, Phase 2 = 1 XL + 1 L + 1 M, Phase 3 = 1 XL + 1 L + 1 M, Phase 4 = 2 M + 1 S.
rejected_alternatives: Phase 0 (no gaps.json found, not needed)
3-phase plan (would combine Phase 1+2 or Phase 3+4, losing logical dependency sequencing)

decisions.rationale: User chose sequence-only roadmap without specifying total program weeks. Phases render with order + dependencies only; duration_weeks omitted from roadmap.json.
rejected_alternatives: Commit to specific week total

decisions.rationale: Roles document identifies expertise required, not FTE counts. Disciplines span architecture (Salesforce Architect), security (FedRAMP/ATO, PCI DSS), development (Apex, LWC, Mobile SDK), data (migration, quality, analytics), integration (SAML, REST, SFTP), training, and program management. Federal government experience required for Security Specialist, Data Migration Lead, Scrum Master.
rejected_alternatives: Provide team sizes or FTE counts (explicitly out of scope per skill guardrail)

open_threads: Duration commitment deferred — user can add total program weeks later and re-run roadmap to allocate proportionally
Strategy skill not run yet (would add personas to quantum-leap bundle)
Quantum-leap bundle generation now unblocked (roadmap.json created)
sessions.next_steps: Quantum-leap skill to generate build-handoff bundle for Meshmesh or Claude Code. Or Validate skill for consistency check before client handoff.

sessions.summary: Generated build-handoff bundle for Phase 1 only (Foundation & Security — E07, E04). Target org: USDA-Recreation-Dev (sandbox). Blueprint: Plan & Build a Sales Cloud Implementation. Bundle includes 9 files (agent brief, engagement intent, glossary, org rules, phase orchestration, epics context, source JSON). Flat-file structure for Meshmesh References panel or Claude Code directory reference.

decisions.rationale: User selected Phase 1 only for first build cycle. Recommended approach: build Phase 1, validate, obtain ATO, then generate Phase 2 bundle. Incremental delivery reduces risk.
rejected_alternatives: All 4 phases (would create 40+ page bundle, harder to review/approve)
Phases 1-2 only

decisions.rationale: Sandbox-first deployment is safer for testing and iterating. Government will promote to production via DevOps Center after Phase 1 acceptance and ATO obtained.
rejected_alternatives: Production (too risky for first build cycle)
Scratch org (less persistent for long Phase 1 ATO process)

decisions.rationale: Closest blueprint match for multi-cloud Salesforce implementation with Platform customization. Confirmed available in Meshmesh docs (fetched https://docs.meshmesh.io/llms-full.txt).
rejected_alternatives: Custom (no blueprint)
Mixed/multi-cloud (no specific multi-cloud blueprint identified in Meshmesh docs)
